text: The `generate` command creates a frozen input spec (input_spec.json) on the first run from a config YAML file.
The `generate` command reads an existing spec from R2 on retry runs when passed a run_id.
The `generate` command errors if `--config` is passed for a run_id that already has a spec in R2.
The `generate` command validates R2 and RunPod credentials before launching any workers.
The `generate` command lists staged shards in `metadata/workers/shards/` to compute the missing shard set.
The `generate` command computes the missing shard set as `spec_shards - staged_valid_shards`.
The `generate` command partitions missing shards across N workers.
The `generate` command submits N worker tasks and exits without waiting for completion.
The `generate` command exits 0 when no shards are missing.
The `generate` command supports `--dry-run` to print shard assignments without launching workers.
The `generate` command uploads a `git diff` to `metadata/run_diff.patch` when `is_repo_dirty` is true.
The `status` command prints reconciliation results without launching workers.
The `status` command does not query RunPod or check worker health.
The `status` command output includes counts for spec shards, staged valid, missing, quarantined, finalized, and worker reports.
The `status` command lists missing shard IDs and quarantined shard IDs individually.
The `status` command overlays worker error details from metadata files when present.
The `finalize` command checks for `dataset.complete` as its first step and exits 0 if `dataset.complete` exists and all canonical outputs are valid.
The `finalize` command deletes a stale `dataset.complete` marker and reruns finalization if the marker exists but outputs are missing or corrupt.
The `finalize` command reports which shards are missing and exits 1 when any shards are absent from staging.
The `finalize` command selects the lexicographically smallest `{worker_id}-{attempt_uuid}` filename when multiple staged attempts exist for the same shard.
The `finalize` command performs a structural check (valid HDF5, expected datasets present, shapes match spec) on each selected staged shard before promotion.
The `finalize` command writes an `.invalid` marker for a staged shard and exits 1 if that shard fails the structural check.
The `finalize` command copies each structurally valid staged shard from `metadata/workers/shards/shard-{id}/{worker}-{attempt}.h5` to `data/shards/shard-{id}.h5`.
The `finalize` command writes a `.promoted` marker for each promoted shard.
The `finalize` command does not delete staged files after promotion.
The `finalize` command downloads canonical shards from `data/shards/` to local storage.
The `finalize` command computes normalization statistics (mean and std) across the training set.
The `finalize` command produces HDF5 virtual datasets `train.h5`, `val.h5`, and `test.h5` when `output_format` is `hdf5`.
The `finalize` command produces WebDataset `.tar` archives per split when `output_format` is `wds`.
Each WebDataset `.tar` shard contains samples as individual files named `{sample_id}.audio.npy`, `{sample_id}.params.npy`, and `{sample_id}.mel.npy`.
The `finalize` command writes `dataset.json` as a self-describing dataset card.
The `finalize` command registers the finalized dataset as a W&B artifact named `data-{dataset_config_id}` of type `"dataset"` in the `synth-setter` project.
The W&B artifact includes `input_spec.json` and `dataset.json` as files.
The W&B artifact includes a reference to the R2 path rather than uploading shard data to W&B.
The `finalize` command writes pipeline metrics to `wandb.summary`.
The `finalize` command initializes W&B with `job_type="data-generation"`.
The `finalize` command uploads the finalized dataset to R2.
The `finalize` command writes `dataset.complete` as the very last step, after all outputs are uploaded.
`dataset.complete` contains the run_id and finalization timestamp.
The `finalize` command restarts from scratch on crash with no partial checkpoints.
The `finalize` command supports `--dry-run` to show what would be downloaded and resharded without executing.
The `finalize` command preserves all quarantined files by default after writing `dataset.complete`.
The `--keep-quarantine-days` flag in `finalize` controls how long quarantined files are retained.
The `finalize` command logs a warning when `worker_architectures` in `dataset.json` contains more than one distinct architecture.
Workers write all output files under `metadata/workers/`.
Workers never write to `data/shards/`.
Finalize is the only pipeline stage that writes to `data/shards/`.
A shard is staged-valid when both a `.h5` file and a `.valid` marker exist in `metadata/workers/shards/shard-{id}/`.
Workers write the `.rendering` marker at the start of a shard attempt.
Workers do not delete the `.rendering` marker when they write the `.valid` marker.
Workers write the `.valid` marker as the last step, only after render, validation, upload, and bookkeeping all complete successfully.
Workers perform full 4-check validation (structural, shape, value, row count) on each shard locally before uploading.
Workers upload a corrupt shard to `metadata/workers/shards/shard-{id}/quarantine/{worker_id}-{attempt_uuid}.h5` on local validation failure.
Workers write an `.invalid` marker on local validation failure.
Workers write a `report.json` file including content hashes, timing, and per-shard results.
Each worker attempt writes its shard to a unique staging filename: `{worker_id}-{attempt_uuid}.h5`.
Workers check the staging directory for an existing valid shard and skip upload if one already exists.
Each shard renders in a separate OS process using `multiprocessing.get_context("spawn")`.
A bash EXIT trap uploads the debug log and a fallback error JSON to R2 when the worker process exits.
Worker debug logs are written in JSONL format to `metadata/workers/attempts/{worker_id}-{attempt}/debug.log`.
Worker reports include `cpu_arch` and `os_info` fields for diagnosing cross-hardware non-determinism.
All rclone operations use `--checksum` to verify transfer integrity.
Content hashes (SHA-256) are recorded per shard in worker reports.
`generate` and `status` reconciliation checks for `.h5` + `.valid` marker existence without loading shard data.
Per-shard seeds are computed as `base_seed + shard_id`.
Shard IDs are logical and deterministic, taking the form `shard-{zero_padded_index}`.
The input spec is written to R2 once on first `generate` and never modified thereafter.
The input spec uses Pydantic `BaseModel` with `strict=True` and `frozen=True`.
The `dataset_wandb_run_id` takes the format `{dataset_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}`.
Config files use YAML format for human-authored content.
Machine-generated structured files (specs, reports, cards) use JSON format.
Debug logs use JSONL format with one event per line.
Lifecycle markers are empty files whose presence encodes state; no content is parsed.
Datasets are immutable once `dataset.complete` exists.
The `dataset.json` card references the input spec by SHA-256 via `input_spec_sha256` and `input_spec_path` fields.
`dataset.json` includes provenance, structure, stats, validation summary, and `worker_architectures`.

Pipeline metrics written to `wandb.summary` include: `pipeline/shards_total`, `pipeline/shards_valid`, `pipeline/shards_quarantined`, `pipeline/total_samples`, `pipeline/generation_time_seconds`, `pipeline/finalize_time_seconds`, and `pipeline/errors_total`.
Workers always produce HDF5 shards regardless of the configured `output_format`.
The `ComputeBackend` protocol defines a single `submit()` method.
The `LocalBackend` runs the worker loop in-process using a local filesystem that mirrors the R2 directory structure.
The `LocalBackend` accepts an optional `generate_fn` callable for test injection.
The `RunPodBackend` wraps the `runpod` Python SDK.
The CLI tags all RunPod instances with the `run_id` at launch.
The `pipeline.cli cleanup` command queries the RunPod API for pods matching a `run_id` and terminates them.
The worker entrypoint uses SIGTERM with a grace period before escalating to SIGKILL for worker timeouts.
Workers use `structlog` with JSON rendering for debug logging.
All transient storage operations use a centralized `tenacity` retry policy with exponential backoff.
The pipeline runs the full workflow locally using `LocalBackend` with a local filesystem for development and testing.
The W&B artifact metadata includes `dataset_config_id`, `dataset_wandb_run_id`, `shard_count`, `param_spec`, `code_version`, `is_repo_dirty`, `total_samples`, and split sizes.
Training runs declare the dataset as a W&B input artifact using `run.use_artifact(f"data-{dataset_config_id}:latest")`.
The `Sample` dataclass used during HDF5-to-WebDataset transcoding uses `frozen=True` and `slots=True`.
The pipeline supports generating datasets at multi-terabyte scale.
The `generate` command deduces `renderer_version` from the VST plugin bundle at spec materialization time.
The R2 root path for a run follows the pattern `data/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/`.
Worker shard staging files live under `metadata/workers/shards/shard-{id}/` and worker attempt artifacts live under `metadata/workers/attempts/{worker_id}-{attempt_uuid}/`.
The evaluation pipeline runs on local macOS developer machines without cluster dependencies.
The evaluation pipeline runs on local Linux machines without cluster dependencies.
The evaluation pipeline runs inside Docker containers.
The evaluation pipeline runs on GitHub Actions CI runners.
No committed config file contains the string '/data/scratch'.
The predict stage loads a trained checkpoint via PyTorch Lightning's Trainer.predict() and writes predicted parameter tensors to disk.
The predict stage outputs files named pred-{batch_idx}.pt, target-audio-{batch_idx}.pt, and target-params-{batch_idx}.pt.
The predict stage resolves dataset path from data.dataset_root.
When data.r2_path is explicitly set, SurgeDataModule.prepare_data() syncs the dataset from R2 before loading data.
When data.r2_path is not set, SurgeDataModule.prepare_data() performs no R2 sync (local-only mode).
data.r2_path has no default value and must be specified explicitly to trigger R2 download.
The predict stage supports the ${wandb:...} OmegaConf resolver for checkpoint path resolution.
The render stage produces sample_{N}/pred.wav, sample_{N}/target.wav, sample_{N}/spec.png, and sample_{N}/params.csv for each sample.
On macOS, the render stage invokes predict_vst_audio.py directly without launching Xvfb.
On headless Linux (DISPLAY unset), renderscript.sh launches Xvfb on :99 and sets the DISPLAY environment variable before invoking predict_vst_audio.py.
renderscript.sh kills the Xvfb process on exit via a trap.
The render stage denormalizes parameters from [-1, 1] to [0, 1] before decoding.
The plugin path defaults to plugins/Surge XT.vst3 and is overridable via --plugin_path.
The preset path defaults to presets/surge-base.vstpreset and is overridable via --preset_path.
The metrics stage computes MSS, wMFCC, SOT, and RMS metrics for each (predicted, target) audio pair.
The metrics stage writes per-sample results to metrics.csv indexed by directory name.
The metrics stage writes aggregated mean and standard deviation across samples to aggregated_metrics.csv.
The metrics stage uses ProcessPoolExecutor for parallel metric computation across samples.
MSS uses three mel-scale windows: 10ms (hop 5ms, 64 mels), 25ms (hop 10ms, 128 mels), and 100ms (hop 50ms, 128 mels), averaged via L1 distance.
wMFCC extracts 13 MFCCs from both signals, computes DTW alignment cost, and weights by frame energy.
SOT normalizes STFT magnitude spectra to probability distributions per frame and computes Wasserstein-1 distance averaged across frames.
RMS computes cosine similarity between RMS energy envelopes in overlapping windows, yielding a value in [-1, 1].
The _wandb_resolver function checks $PROJECT_ROOT/.cache/checkpoints/ for a cached artifact before calling the W&B API.
The _wandb_resolver function downloads the artifact via wandb.Api().artifact().download() when no cached copy exists.
The _wandb_resolver function returns the local path to the downloaded checkpoint without modifying src/eval.py or src/train.py.
The wandb OmegaConf resolver is registered in src/utils/utils.py via register_resolvers() and is only registered if not already present.
The ${wandb:...} resolver resolves lazily — the W&B API is called only on first access of cfg.ckpt_path.
configs/logger/wandb.yaml sets log_model to 'all', uploading every checkpoint saved by ModelCheckpoint.
configs/logger/wandb.yaml sets entity to ${oc.env:WANDB_ENTITY,tinaudio} and project to ${oc.env:WANDB_PROJECT,synth-setter}.
All rclone operations include the --checksum flag.
rclone_sync() raises subprocess.CalledProcessError on failure.
rclone_sync() supports a dry-run mode via --dry-run flag passthrough.
R2 configuration is read from environment variables (RCLONE_CONFIG_R2_*).
R2 dataset sync runs in prepare_data() before setup() is called.
R2 dataset sync is a no-op when local data already matches the remote checksum.
R2 dataset sync logs download progress via structlog.

make upload-eval executes rclone sync with --checksum from the local eval output directory to the R2 path r2:synth-data/eval/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/{train_config_id}/{train_wandb_run_id}/{eval_config_id}/{eval_wandb_run_id}/.
make upload-eval is not triggered automatically — it requires explicit invocation.
The W&B eval run declares model checkpoint and dataset as input artifacts via use_artifact().
The W&B eval run logs summary metrics mss, wmfcc, sot, and rms.
The W&B eval run creates an eval artifact with an R2 reference using the s3:// protocol.
The W&B eval run sets job_type to 'evaluation'.
make eval executes predict, render, and metrics stages in sequence.
make predict invokes python src/eval.py with mode=predict.
make render invokes renderscript.sh on Linux or predict_vst_audio.py directly on macOS.
make metrics invokes python scripts/compute_audio_metrics.py.
make docker-eval runs the full predict → render → metrics pipeline inside a Docker container.
make upload-eval syncs eval artifacts to R2 using rclone with --checksum.
make help lists all available targets.
configs/data/surge_simple.yaml sets dataset_root to ${paths.data_dir}/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id} with no hardcoded absolute path.
configs/data/surge_mini.yaml sets dataset_root using the same paths convention as surge_simple.yaml.
.env.example contains only R2 credentials and WANDB_API_KEY — no path variables.
.env is not committed to the repository.
The predict stage fails fast with a clear error message when the dataset is not found at dataset_root.
Experiment configs pin a checkpoint via ${wandb:entity/project/artifact-name:alias} syntax.
ckpt_path in configs/eval.yaml is set to ??? so that Hydra errors if no checkpoint is specified.
ckpt_path in configs/train.yaml defaults to null to start training fresh.
The Docker eval image has Xvfb baked in for headless rendering.
The Docker eval image has the Surge XT plugin installed.
The Docker eval container mounts .env for credentials only and passes output paths as docker run arguments.
The E2E CI workflow runs on pull requests when src/eval.py, scripts/, or configs/ are changed.
The E2E CI workflow uses only checked-in fixtures and requires no R2 credentials or network access.
The CI fixture set includes a checkpoint of approximately 1 MB, an HDF5 shard of approximately 5 MB with 10–50 samples, and pre-rendered audio for at least one sample.
The E2E CI test validates that prediction files exist, audio renders successfully, and the metrics CSV matches the expected schema.
If CI fixtures exceed approximately 10 MB, they are migrated to Git LFS.
The 19 SGE scripts in jobs/predict/ are left in the repository without modification and receive no future maintenance.
No new code references SGE directives or SGE job submission commands.
src/data/rclone.py exposes rclone_sync(), rclone_ls(), and rclone_copyto() functions.
A fresh clone of the repository produces a working metrics.csv on a small fixture in under 15 minutes on a developer laptop.
scripts/compute_audio_metrics.py accepts a --wandb-run flag that logs metrics to a W&B run when provided.
The local eval output directory structure follows the path logs/eval/{train_config_id}/{train_wandb_run_id}/{dataset_config_id}/{eval_wandb_run_id}/.
The R2 eval artifact path follows the six-segment convention: eval/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/{train_config_id}/{train_wandb_run_id}/{eval_config_id}/{eval_wandb_run_id}/.
Audio for the metrics stage is loaded via pedalboard.io.AudioFile at the file's native sample rate.
The checkpoint cache directory $PROJECT_ROOT/.cache/checkpoints/ is gitignored.
The wandb OmegaConf resolver safe-names the artifact reference by replacing '/' and ':' with '_' when constructing the cache directory name.
The W&B eval run config records dataset_config_id, dataset_wandb_run_id, train_config_id, train_wandb_run_id, eval_config_id, eval_wandb_run_id, and github_sha.
Every make target is safe to re-run (idempotent).
Rendering only processes audio files that are missing from the output directory.
Metrics are only recomputed when inputs change.
`dataset_config_id` is derived from the filename stem of the YAML config file under `configs/dataset/`.
`train_config_id` is derived from the filename stem of the YAML config file under `configs/experiment/`.
`eval_config_id` is derived from the filename stem of the eval dataset YAML config file under `configs/dataset/`.
`dataset_wandb_run_id` defaults to `{dataset_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}` when not explicitly configured.
`train_wandb_run_id` defaults to `{train_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}` when not explicitly configured.
`eval_wandb_run_id` defaults to `{eval_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}` when not explicitly configured.
Each `*_wandb_run_id` is set via `wandb.init(id=...)`.
The default W&B run ID timestamp uses the format `YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ` (seconds precision, UTC, filesystem-safe).
W&B run IDs must not exceed 64 characters.
Config filenames must be kept short enough that the resulting W&B run ID stays within 64 characters.
Individual design docs must not define their own storage or provenance conventions; they must reference this spec instead.
Data generation outputs reside under `synth-data/data/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/` in the R2 bucket.
Training outputs reside under `synth-data/train/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/{train_config_id}/{train_wandb_run_id}/` in the R2 bucket.
Evaluation outputs reside under `synth-data/eval/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/{train_config_id}/{train_wandb_run_id}/{eval_config_id}/{eval_wandb_run_id}/` in the R2 bucket.
Workers write only under `metadata/workers/` within the data generation path.
The finalize stage exclusively writes to `shards/` within the data generation path.
All `rclone` operations use the `--checksum` flag.
Datasets become immutable once `metadata/dataset.complete` exists.
Creating a new dataset version requires a new `dataset_wandb_run_id`.
The data generation path stores a frozen pipeline config provenance copy at `metadata/config.yaml`.
The data generation path stores the frozen input specification at `metadata/input_spec.json`.
The data generation path stores the self-describing dataset card at `metadata/dataset.json`.
The data generation path stores the completion marker at `metadata/dataset.complete`.
Worker shard staging files are stored at `metadata/workers/shards/{shard_id}/{worker_id}-{attempt_uuid}.*` within the data generation path.
Worker attempt reports are stored at `metadata/workers/attempts/{worker_id}-{attempt_uuid}/report.json` within the data generation path.
Split virtual datasets `train.h5`, `val.h5`, and `test.h5` reside in the data generation path root.
The normalization statistics file `stats.npz` resides in the data generation path root.
Training checkpoints reside under `checkpoints/` within the training path.
The training checkpoint directory contains `last.ckpt` and `best.ckpt`.
The training path stores the frozen experiment config at `config.yaml`.
Evaluation prediction tensors are stored under `predictions/` within the eval path, named `pred-{batch_idx}.pt`, `target-audio-{batch_idx}.pt`, and `target-params-{batch_idx}.pt`.
Evaluation audio samples are stored under `audio/{sample_N}/` within the eval path.
Each evaluation audio sample directory contains `pred.wav`, `target.wav`, `spec.png`, and `params.csv`.
Evaluation metrics are stored at `metrics/metrics.csv` and `metrics/aggregated_metrics.csv` within the eval path.
The dataset W&B artifact uses type `dataset` and the name pattern `data-{dataset_config_id}`.
`pipeline.cli finalize` logs the dataset W&B artifact.
The model W&B artifact uses type `model` and the name pattern `model-{train_config_id}`.
`src/train.py` logs the model W&B artifact.
The eval-results W&B artifact uses type `eval-results` and the name pattern `eval-{eval_config_id}`.
The eval script logs the eval-results W&B artifact.
Each new run of the same config produces the next W&B artifact version (`:v0`, `:v1`, `:v2`, etc.).
The `*_wandb_run_id` is stored in `artifact.metadata`, not in the artifact name.
W&B automatically sets the `:latest` alias on every `log_artifact` call.
The training script sets the `:best` alias on the model artifact when the validation metric improves.
The promote workflow sets the `:production` alias on the model artifact in W&B when the model is promoted to a GitHub Release.
Every run calls `run.use_artifact()` for each input artifact to establish lineage links.
Every run calls `run.log_artifact()` for each output artifact.
Runs use `run.use_artifact()` instead of `api.artifact()` for input artifacts, because only `run.use_artifact()` creates W&B lineage links.
Every run includes `github_sha` in `wandb.config`.
`wandb.config` stores hyperparameters — values set before the run.
`wandb.summary` stores final metrics — values measured after the run.
`artifact.metadata` stores properties of the artifact itself.
Dataset properties are stored on the dataset artifact, not on the training runs that consume it.
Every `wandb.init()` call sets the `job_type` parameter.
The data generation stage sets `job_type="data-generation"` in `wandb.init()`.
The training stage sets `job_type="training"` in `wandb.init()`.
The evaluation stage sets `job_type="evaluation"` in `wandb.init()`.
The Tests workflow (`test.yml`) triggers on push and pull request events.
The Tests workflow runs on `ubuntu-latest` and `macos-latest` runners.
The Full Tests workflow (`test-expensive.yml`) triggers on push to the main branch and on `workflow_dispatch`.
The Full Tests workflow runs on the `gpu-x64` runner.
The Data Generation workflow triggers via `workflow_dispatch` and requires R2, W&B, and RunPod secrets.
The Data Generation workflow accepts `config` and `n_workers` as key inputs.
The Training workflow triggers via `workflow_dispatch` and requires R2, W&B, and RunPod secrets.
The Training workflow accepts `experiment` and `overrides` as key inputs.
The Evaluation workflow triggers via `workflow_dispatch` and requires R2 and W&B secrets.
The Evaluation workflow requires `train_wandb_run_id` and `eval_config_id` as inputs.
The Model Promotion workflow file is `promote.yml` and triggers via `workflow_dispatch`.
The Model Promotion workflow requires W&B and `GITHUB_TOKEN` secrets.
The Model Promotion workflow accepts `train_wandb_run_id`, `eval_wandb_run_id`, `registry`, and `dry_run` as key inputs.
All workflows that create W&B runs export `GITHUB_SHA` into the run environment.
The Promotion workflow pulls the model artifact from the training run identified by `train_wandb_run_id`.
The Promotion workflow pulls eval metrics from the eval run identified by `eval_wandb_run_id`.
The Promotion workflow creates a GitHub Release with tag format `model-v{N}` using an incrementing integer.
The Promotion workflow attaches the model file (`.pt` or `.onnx`) as an asset to the GitHub Release.
The GitHub Release body includes training metrics from the training run's `wandb.summary`, excluding underscore-prefixed fields.
The GitHub Release body includes eval metrics from the eval run's `wandb.summary`, excluding underscore-prefixed fields.
The GitHub Release body includes the full training config as JSON.
The GitHub Release body includes dataset artifact information from the training run's `used_artifacts()`.
The GitHub Release body includes eval dataset artifact information from the eval run's `used_artifacts()`.
The GitHub Release body includes a link to the W&B training run.
The GitHub Release body includes a link to the W&B evaluation run.
The GitHub Release body includes the UTC date/timestamp of the promotion.
The GitHub Release body includes the git SHA of the commit that produced the training run.
Secrets are available only to the workflows that require them.
The training workflow requires R2 read access to download dataset shards.
The training workflow requires R2 write access to upload checkpoints.
The W&B entity is `tinaudio`.
The W&B project is `synth-setter`.
The W&B entity is configured via the `WANDB_ENTITY` environment variable.
The W&B project is configured via the `WANDB_PROJECT` environment variable.
Hydra configs use `${oc.env:WANDB_ENTITY,tinaudio}` for the entity field.
Hydra configs use `${oc.env:WANDB_PROJECT,synth-setter}` for the project field.
Legacy runs under `benhayes`/`synth-permutations` are treated as read-only.
New runs use the `tinaudio` entity and `synth-setter` project.
W&B artifacts reference R2 objects via `artifact.add_reference("s3://synth-data/...")`.
Any environment that calls `add_reference` or downloads reference artifacts must set `AWS_ENDPOINT_URL` or `WANDB_S3_ENDPOINT_URL` to the R2 endpoint.
Artifacts store metadata, manifests, and statistics — not large bulk data files.
Each `*_wandb_run_id` uniquely identifies an immutable run output.
All configs are frozen into the artifact or R2 storage path at run time.
R2 paths are append-only after their completion markers exist.
Runs do not consume data from R2 paths that lack a completion marker.
Training runs on local Linux/macOS, Docker, and RunPod environments.
Checkpoints survive pod termination by persisting as W&B model artifacts via `log_model="all"`.
Resume restores full optimizer, scheduler, and epoch state from the last durable checkpoint.
Every training run records dataset version, training config, git SHA, and checkpoint lineage in W&B.
Training executes as a single long-running job with a single launcher and a single resume path.
Training does not use the data pipeline's reconciliation backend.
`WandbLogger` is configured with `log_model="all"` to upload every checkpoint as a W&B model artifact.
`WandbLogger` is configured with `project="synth-setter"`.
Checkpoint upload to W&B runs as a background process and does not block the training loop.
Checkpoint durability requires no custom callback code beyond configuring `log_model="all"` on `WandbLogger`.
Dataset root resolves to `${paths.data_dir}/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}`.
Training uses the local dataset by default without fetching from R2.
Training syncs the dataset from R2 before starting when `r2_path` is set in the config.
`ckpt_path=` accepts both local file paths and `wandb:` artifact references.
When `ckpt_path=` is a `wandb:` artifact reference, the system downloads it to a local cache before passing the local path to Lightning.
Resume logic delegates entirely to Lightning's native checkpoint resume semantics.
`make resume EXPERIMENT=<name> RUN_ID=<id>` resolves the W&B artifact and initiates resume without manual path assembly.
Every training run sets `job_type="training"` in the W&B run.
Every training run calls `run.use_artifact()` for the input dataset artifact.
Every training run calls `run.log_artifact()` for the output model artifact.
Every training run writes `github_sha` to `wandb.config`.
Model artifact names follow the pattern `model-{train_config_id}`.
Dataset artifact names follow the pattern `data-{dataset_config_id}`.
Training writes final metrics to `wandb.summary`.
Training does not create GitHub Releases.
Promotion runs as a separate, manually triggered workflow after training completes.
RunPod training is launched by a thin script that creates a single pod; it is not a backend abstraction.
The training Docker image is separate from the data-generation Docker image.
`last.ckpt` is stored locally and uploaded to W&B as a model artifact.
`best.ckpt` is stored locally and uploaded to W&B as a model artifact.
Intermediate step checkpoints are uploaded to W&B as model artifacts.
Training does not upload checkpoints to R2 in v1.
Pod failure causes loss of at most one checkpoint interval of training progress.
A CI smoke test verifies that a training run with a small fixture dataset reaches at least one checkpoint and exits cleanly.
`make train EXPERIMENT=<name>` launches local training.
`make runpod-train EXPERIMENT=<name>` launches training on RunPod.
`make docker-train EXPERIMENT=<name>` launches training inside a Docker container.
Training emits metrics to W&B and writes CSV logs as a local fallback.
`train_wandb_run_id` defaults to the format `{train_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}`.
Multi-node distributed training is not supported.
Sweep orchestration is not supported.
Automatic model promotion after training is not supported.
The entrypoint (scripts/docker_entrypoint.sh) dispatches behavior based on the MODE environment variable.
The entrypoint errors when MODE is unset.
The entrypoint errors when MODE is set to an unrecognized value.
When MODE=idle, the entrypoint executes `sleep infinity` and ignores any arguments.
When MODE=passthrough and arguments are provided, the entrypoint executes those arguments via exec.
When MODE=passthrough and no arguments are provided, the entrypoint exits with code 0.
When MODE=generate_dataset, the entrypoint runs VST dataset generation via `pipeline.entrypoints.generate_dataset` under headless X11.
The MODE=generate_dataset mode accepts configuration exclusively via environment variables, not CLI arguments.
docker/ubuntu22_04/Dockerfile defines three build targets selectable via --target: prod, dev-snapshot, and dev-live.
The prod target uses docker_entrypoint.sh as its entrypoint.
The prod target bakes source code at GIT_REF using a tarball.
The dev-snapshot target uses docker_entrypoint.sh as its entrypoint.
The dev-snapshot target clones source code via git at GIT_REF.
The dev-live target uses a fallback entrypoint that produces an error when no source volume is mounted.
The dev-live target uses volume-mounted source code.
All image targets inherit from the r2-config-base stage.
R2 credentials are baked into the image only when BuildKit secrets are provided at build time.
When BuildKit secrets are not provided at build time, the image uses a placeholder rclone config.
WANDB_API_KEY is required at runtime; the image does not bake W&B authentication.
The IMAGE build ARG defaults to dev-snapshot and selects the final build target.
The SYNTH_PERMUTATIONS_GIT_REF build ARG defaults to main and specifies the git ref for source code.
The SURGE_GIT_REF build ARG defaults to a pinned SHA and specifies the Surge XT release commit.
The BUILD_MODE build ARG defaults to source and accepts source or prebuilt as the Surge install method.
The R2_BUCKET build ARG defaults to empty and specifies the Cloudflare R2 bucket name.
The TORCH_INDEX_URL build ARG is required and has no default value.
The SYNTH_PERMUTATIONS_GIT_REF environment variable is baked into the prod and dev-snapshot targets and holds the git ref used at build time.
The R2_BUCKET environment variable is baked into all targets via r2-config-base and holds the Cloudflare R2 bucket name.
The VIRTUAL_ENV environment variable is set to /venv/main in all targets.
The PATH environment variable is set to $VIRTUAL_ENV/bin:$PATH in all targets.
DATASET_CONFIG is required when MODE=generate_dataset and specifies the path to a dataset config YAML inside the container.
RUN_METADATA_DIR defaults to /run-metadata when MODE=generate_dataset and specifies the directory where input_spec.json is written.
In MODE=generate_dataset, the container materializes a DatasetPipelineSpec.
In MODE=generate_dataset, the container uploads the spec and shard to R2.
In MODE=generate_dataset, the entrypoint writes input_spec.json to RUN_METADATA_DIR.
In MODE=generate_dataset, the entrypoint generates shard_size samples and produces exactly one shard per invocation.
In MODE=generate_dataset, setting num_shards greater than 1 raises NotImplementedError.
